title: Non GATE CSE - Computer Graphics - Line Drawing Algorithm

questions.question:
Consider the Breshenham’s line generation algorithm for a line with gradient greater than one, current point \( (x_{ i} , y_{i} ) \) and decision parameter, \( d_{i} \). The next point to be plotted \( (x _{i+1}, y_{i+1}) \) and updated decision parameter, \( d_{i+1} \), for \( d_{i} < 0 \) are given as _______.



Computer Graphics: UGC NET CSE | August 2016 | Part 3 | Question: 15
options: \( x _{i+1} = x_{ i}  y_{i+1} = y_{i + 1}  d_{i+1} = d_{i} + 2 (dx – dy) \)

questions.question:
The Liang-Barsky line clipping algorithm uses the parametric equation of a line from \( (x_{1} , y_{1} ) \) to \( (x_{2} , y_{2} ) \) along with its infinite extension which is given as :

\( x = x_{1} + \Delta x.u \)

\( y = y_{1} + \Delta y.u \) 

Where \( \Delta x = x_{2} – x_{1} , \Delta y = y_{2} – y_{1} \), and \( u \) is the parameter with \( 0 \leq u \leq 1 \). A line \( AB \) with end points \( A(–1, 7) \) and \( B(11, 1) \) is to be clipped against a rectangular window with \( x_{min} = 1, x_{max} = 9, y_{min} = 2 \), and \( y_{max} = 8 \). The lower and upper bound values of the parameter u for the clipped line using Liang-Barsky algorithm is given as :



Computer Graphics: UGC NET CSE | August 2016 | Part 3 | Question: 18
